rag_rules: Assume statements must be placed in component type scopes, not implementation scopes
Guarantee expressions must reference only visible identifiers from the current component scope
Implementation-level bindings and assignments must be placed in implementation annexes
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must use only visible identifiers and valid type expressions
Component type contracts may not reference implementation-specific subcomponents or ports
Generated AGREE code must preserve type consistency and avoid unresolved references
Validator diagnostics pointing to implementation scope errors should be resolved by moving or removing clauses
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Assume and guarantee clauses must be valid in their respective component type or implementation scopes
Expression types must match expected boolean, integer, or real types in AGREE
References to ports, subcomponents, and data types must be resolvable within the current AADL scope
anti_patterns: Adding assume statements in implementation annexes instead of component type annexes
Referencing unresolved identifiers or invalid helper functions in AGREE expressions
Attempting to define new architecture elements in AGREE contracts during repair
irrelevant_topics: AADL package references and with clauses
AGREE model fusion and owner/scope rules
